rulings.Case Title: DENIS NEEMA VS. SALESFORCE.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings Of Defendants Salesforce.Com, Inc. And Antonio Akins
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANTONIO AKINS, SALESFORCE.COM, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings Of Defendants Salesforce.Com, Inc. And Antonio Akins Defendants Salesforce.com, Inc. and Antonio Akins' motion to compel arbitration and to stay proceedings is GRANTED. The arbitration agreement covers Plaintiff Denis Neema's claims and the agreement is not unconscionable. The agreement clearly emphasized the ability to opt out of arbitration and seven days was sufficient time for Plaintiff to carefully review the four-page document. The requirement that the arbitration proceedings be confidential is not unconscionable. (Sanchez v. Carmax Auto Superstores California, LLC (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 398, 408.) While Defendant Akins was not a party to the agreement, Defendants may rely on admissions of fact from Plaintiff's complaint to show that Akins was acting as an agent of Salesforce at all relevant times and should therefore be brought into arbitration as well. (See 24 Hour Fitness, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 1199, 1210-11 ("While these [individual] defendants are not parties to the contract between Munshaw and Nautilus, they are entitled to the benefit of the arbitration agreement if, as the complaint alleges, they were acting as agents for Nautilus.").) Finally, Plaintiff's waiver argument fails because Defendants moved to compel arbitration less than three months after being served with the complaint. While Defendants had actual notice of Plaintiff's claims over two years before they filed the motion to compel arbitration, actual notice without proper service is not enough to confer jurisdiction upon the court such that it could hear a motion to compel arbitration. (See Am. Express Centurion Bank v. Zara (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 383, 392 ("Actual notice of the action alone, however, is not a substitute for proper service and is not sufficient to confer jurisdiction.").) The litigation has not advanced in any way to suggest that Plaintiff will be prejudiced by switching to arbitration. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: LANCE JENSEN VS. CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant California Physicians Service Dba Blue Shield Of Californias Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2018, Line 3, Defendant California Physicians Service Dba Blue Shield Of California's Motion For Summary Adjudication Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is OFF CALENDAR as moot. "Because there is but one complaint in a civil action [citation], the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint¿Thus, once an amended complaint is filed, it is error to grant summary adjudication on a cause of action contained in a previous complaint." (State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1131.) On October 23, 2018, the court gave plaintiff leave to file an amended complaint by October 31, 2018. Defendant may renew the motion against the amended complaint if appropriate. Alternatively, if the parties stipulate to the instant motion being heard in December and the case is not designated complex, Department 302 will hear the motion in December. If the parties wish to have the motion heard in December, then they shall meet and confer and immediately inform the court of the date they desire a hearing. If the court does not receive a December date, then the motion will go off calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: WENDY HOUGH ET AL VS. TODD COURAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify Subpoena For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 5, 2018, Line 3, DEFENDANTS TODD COURAGE, MARK HAMILTON, CHRISTOPHER SCORFINA, LYNNE SCHWARTZHOFF'S Motion To Modify Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AAMCO TRANSMISSIONS, INC. VS. ZAPMAN DISTRIBUTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT [Civ. Proc. Code § 437c]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF AAMCO TRANSMISSIONS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT [Civ. Proc. Code § 437c] Plaintiff Aamco's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Aamco's claim is barred by the doctrines of unclean hands/economic duress. Aamco (as assignee) is subject to any defense that defendants could have raised against the assignor. (See YDM Management Co. Inc. v. Sharp Community Medical Group, Inc. (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 613, 621 fn. 3.) In addition, Aamco is subject to the unclean hands defense based on the alleged wrongdoing of its agent, Mr. O'Donnell. (See Restatement 2nd of Contracts § 336(4).) Here, Mr. O'Donnell's statement that defendants' proposed franchise "more than likely" would be subject to the single point scheme was negligent and misleading. Defendants' evidence shows that Mr. O'Donnell had the precise A.C. Nielsen information easily available but failed to disclose it to defendants. (Szupello Dec., par. 17; O'Donnell Depo., 23:3-24:14; Phillips Depo., 24:24-27:12; 29:2-30:23.) Mr. O'Donnell should have provided the precise information or said nothing misleading at all. The unclean hands defense does not require a showing of fraud. "Any conduct that violates conscience, or good faith, or other equitable standards of conduct is sufficient cause to invoke the doctrine." (Kendall-Jackson Winery, Ltd. v. Sup. Crt. (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 970, 979.) The unclean hands defense is available to an action at law. (Id. at 978.) The court rejects plaintiff's argument that Mr. O'Donnell's statement was mere opinion. First, as noted, the unclean hands defense is a broad equitable doctrine that does not require "fraud." Second, Mr. O'Donnell's statement is analogous to statements that have supported fraud/negligent misrepresentation claims. (See Hobart v. Hobart Estate Co. (1945) 26 Cal.2d 412, 433-434 ("This court has held that a false statement of opinion fraudulently made may form the basis of an action where the party making it possesses superior knowledge or special information regarding the subject matter of the representation."); Edward Barron Estate Co. v. Woodruff Co. (1912) 163 Cal. 561, 574 (statement by architect that a building will not cost more than a certain amount may be regarded as an affirmation of fact.).) Lastly, on a related note, the economic duress defense is also viable in the instant circumstances. (See Rich & Whillock, Inc. v. Aston Development, Inc. (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 1154, 1158.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb

rulings.Case Title: SILVER 8 PARTNERS, LP VS. WILLIAM J. O'BRIEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 5, 2018, Line 7, DEFENDANT WILLIAM O'BRIEN's Motion For Sanctions Defendant William J. O'Brien's motion for sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff Silver 8 Partners, LP's interpretation of the facts is not so untenable as to warrant sanctions. It is not clear based on the legal authority and evidence cited by Defendant that Plaintiff's suit is brought in bad faith or is "totally and completely without merit." (CCP § 128.5.) It is not unreasonable or frivolous for Plaintiff to assert that an enforceable contract existed on August 5, 2017 and that it believed the August 19, 2017 letter memorialized discussions about assurances sought regarding Mr. O'Brien's ability to perform on the agreement, rather than modified the prior oral agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/srb
